name: Chip
summary: Compact label, status indicator, or filter affordance. Typography spec: Inter 500, 12/16, 0.16px. Status chips use the 160p/190p tint recipe — background `shades-190-p`, content `shades-160-p`. Decorative category chips use `primary` / `secondary` palettes; status meaning belongs to `error`/`warning`/`info`/`success`.

anatomy.description: Rounded surface with `shades-190-p` background (status) or `primary.shades-12-p` (selected filter).
Optional MUI icon at 16/20px in the same color as the label.
Optional dismissable affordance; click target ≥ 24×24 inside chip.

props.name: onDelete
props.description: Renders the delete icon when provided.

props.name: onClick
props.description: Makes the chip act as a button/toggle.

states.name: hover
states.description: State-layer overlay on the chip surface.

states.name: selected
states.description: Filled `primary.shades-12-p` background, `primary.main` text — only when used as a filter toggle.

a11y: If the chip is interactive (filter/toggle), it must be reachable by Tab and operable via Enter/Space; otherwise it's a non-interactive label.
Status chips must include text — never indicate state via color alone.
The delete icon needs an accessible name (e.g., `aria-label="Remove"`).

examples.label: Status chip (success)
examples.snippet:
import { Chip } from '@mui/material';

<Chip label="Active" color="success" variant="filled" size="small" />

doNot: Use a chip as the principal action of a view — that's a Button's job.
Render a status chip in `secondary` (orange) to mean caution — caution belongs to `warning` via tint recipe.
Skip the label and rely on a color-only chip.
Stack >7 filter chips visible at once — group, hide behind a 'more' menu, or move to a multiselect.
